channel.title: Economy News, Latest Economic News Today | The HinduBusinessLine
channel.description: Economy News Today : Get the Latest news updates on Economy, GDP News, Indian Economy, World Economy, Economic Indicators, Government Policy for Economy on BusinessLine.

image.title: Economy News, Latest Economic News Today | The HinduBusinessLine

item.title: French shipping giant CMA CGM flags container ship in India
item.description: In addition to its first local flag registration in India, the French company will register three more vessels in the coming months, underscoring its commitment to India and its ambition to further develop its presence in the country

item.title: Piyush Goyal, UK counterpart Reynolds meet to advance FTA talks
item.description: Indian Minister on a two-day official trip to London to strengthen bilateral economic ties

item.title: India hopes to pursue social security pact with US simultaneously with trade deal
item.description: Wants US to address work visa concerns as two sides get ready for BTA negotiations

item.title: West Asian airlines protest Mumbai airport’s ‘unilateral’ plan to cut slots
item.description: Mumbai Airport and West Asian carriers clash over winter flight schedule cuts, sparking protests and complaints to Civil Aviation Ministry

item.title: India prepares ground to curb GM alfalfa seed import as US seeks market access
item.description: Scientists may have to develop a kit at short notice to identify transgenic seeds

item.title: Standalone energy storage systems dominate utility-scale tenders in Q1 2025
item.description: A major driver behind the surge is the government’s Viability Gap Funding (VGF) scheme, offering up to 30 per cent capital expenditure support

item.title: Centre seeks feedback from airlines on Pak airspace closure
item.description: The Pakistan air corridor is vital for Indian airlines, especially for west-bound flights from northern cities like Delhi to Europe, the Middle East, Central Asia and even the US

item.title: Record Myanmar crop, higher rabi and zaid output to weigh on urad prices
item.description: Prices down by 25% since November; Imports rose 32 per cent in FY24-25 to over 8.23 lakh tonnes

item.title: India inks deal with France for 26 Rafale-marine fighter jets
item.description: The deal follows the approval given by the Cabinet Committee on Security (CCS), headed by Prime Minister Narendra Modi earlier this month, for the procurement of the naval fighter jet

item.title: Govt slashes floor revenue, zero upfront payment to encourage underground coal mining
item.description: India wants to enhance production from underground mines to 10% of the country’s total output

item.title: Chennai Metro Rail begins trial run on 10-km stretch from Poonamallee to Porur with driverless train set
item.description: The trials involved driverless train set, but operated with drivers, said sources

item.title: Port union extend support to nation-wide strike on May 20
item.description: WTWFI joins nationwide strike against proposed labour codes, urging government to withdraw amendments

item.title: FarMart launches its consumer pantry range in India
item.description: 27 pantry staples, including chakki atta, millet attas and Basmati rice will be available under the brand

item.title: Retail industry clocked 6% growth in March: RAI Survey
item.description: The growth was led by food and grocery and QSR segments

item.title: India’s factory output grew by 3% in March
item.description: For the full fiscal year 2024-25 (FY25), industrial production registered an average growth of 4% versus 5.9% in FY24

item.title: Bought leaf tea manufacturers form nation-wide federation
item.description: Nilgiris Bought Leaf Tea Manufacturers Association, North Bengal Tea Producers Welfare Association, and Assam Bought Leaf Tea Manufacturers Association join hands

item.title: India signs $7.4 billion deal to buy 26 Rafale fighter jets, official says
item.description: The deal comes as India seeks to modernise its military, reduce dependence on Russian-origin equipment, and boost domestic weapons production to supply forces deployed along two contentious borders with Pakistan and China

item.title: US tariffs may erode ₹2700-4500 crore of operating profits of Indian auto component exporters: ICRA
item.description: ICRA projects that revenue growth for the Indian auto component sector, represented by a sample of 46 key players with combined annual revenues of over ₹3 lakh crore in FY2024, could moderate to 6-8% in FY2026, down from the earlier forecast of 8-10%

item.title: Amul targets 5-fold growth in protein-production; pushes protein consumption through IPL tournament
item.description: With 15 products in its protein range, Amul is pushing to make protein products a part of everyday life of Indians.

item.title: India’s dry fruits likely to get costlier due to border closure with Pakistan
item.description: Inflow of dried apricots, almonds, black and green raisins, pistachios, walnuts from Afghanistan may be affected

item.title: Employees hold tight to current jobs as economic jitters shake up hiring
item.description: In a further sign of caution, candidates are increasingly willing to accept smaller salary hikes and, in some cases, even salary cuts, as they prioritise job security over aggressive career moves

item.title: MFIs to implement revised guidelines for pricing of loan, exposure limit from June 1
item.description: No fresh loan to be given to the same borrower, whose existing loan is still continuing as outstanding, before 12 months of disbursement of the existing loan or has not completed 50 per cent repayment of the same

item.title: Chinese firms turn to Indian exporters to help fill US orders
item.description: Chinese exports to the US face high tariffs, while Indian goods are taxed at a lower rate

item.title: Suspension of trade to hurt Pakistan much more than India
item.description: In 2019, Pak allowed imports of pharma within a month of suspension, more products allowed later

item.title: No headway yet on PM E-DRIVE’s electric ambulances
item.description: Maruti Suzuki India says it is in the process of manufacturing

item.title: India to not allow wheat export even if govt procurement exceeds target
item.description: The government had sold about 3 mt of wheat in open market through auction and by releasing to cooperatives in 2024-25

item.title: India is a natural choice for investors seeking long term value and opportunity: RBI Guv Malhotra
item.description: The country is poised to become the third largest economy shortly, says RBI Guv Malhotra

item.title: EPFO hands over appointment letters to 976 recruits at 15th Rozgar Mela
item.description: The event saw the distribution of over 51,000 appointment letters to newly inducted youth in various government departments, including EPFO

item.title: Indian Navy carries out anti-ship firings; demonstrates readiness for long-range 'offensive' strikes
item.description: The demonstration of the Navy's combat prowess came amid heightened tensions between India and Pakistan over the dastardly Pahalgam terror attack that killed 26 civilians

item.title: Power, Environment Ministries ask CPCB to examine relevance of FGD in coal thermal plants
item.description: They have recommended against FGD installation in Indian thermal power plants citing low sulfur sulphur coal, National Ambient Air Quality Standards compliance, and minimal impact on PM reduction

item.title: Biogas sector gets ₹200 cr investment commitments at RenewX 2025: IBA
item.description: The three-day expo saw investment commitments of ₹200 crore through various MoUs in the biogas industry

item.title: RBI Governor Malhotra asks US industry to invest in India
item.description: India recorded an average annual growth rate of 8.2% over past four years, Governor Sanjay Malhotra says

item.title: Steel Ministry mandates sector-wide data reporting under new policy push, penalties for non-compliance
item.description: Notice to be issued by statistics officer to informants, indicating the date, format and manner in which to furnish information

item.title: India seeks new trade opportunities with Croatia and Netherlands to diversify trade partnerships
item.description: Commerce Secretary Sunil Barthwal and Croatia’s State Secretary Zdenko Lucic discuss boosting trade and investment ties

item.title: Agriculture insurance in India: Securing the future of our farmers
item.description: With the right blend of policy, technology and empathy, agri-insurance can be more than a safety net

item.title: Transforming India’s agricultural insurance for a resilient future
item.description: Expanding coverage to livestock, equipment, and emerging sectors to secure farmers’ livelihoods and Ddrive sustainable growth

item.title: Maharashtra govt planning water metro for Mumbai, Kochi body to help with DPR: Nitesh Rane
item.description: DPR expected by end of the month, Maharashtra ports minister Nitesh Rane says

item.title: Pak airspace closure: Centre asks airlines to bolster passenger facilities
item.description: Airlines instructed to enhance passenger amenities and handling protocols due to Pakistan airspace closures, impacting flight routes and services

item.title: Amul launches high-protein Kulfi
item.description: Launched in 2022, this range comprises India’s first plain lactose free Whey Protein Concentrate with 25 gram protein in a single serve sachet of 32 grams

item.title: Indian consumers’ interest in gold likely to continue during Akshaya Tritiya, despite prices ruling near ₹1 lakh/10 gm
item.description: Bullion industry wooing buyers with advance booking, discounts and lower making charges

item.title: Centre says media platforms should refrain from live telecast of defence operations
item.description: The Ministry added that “all stakeholders are requested to continue exercising vigilance, sensitivity, and responsibility in coverage, upholding the highest standards in the service of the nation”

item.title: India urging firms to acquire overseas iron ore, coking coal assets, official says
item.description: ‘We are encouraging our companies to acquire assets abroad, right from iron ore to coking coal to even limestone and dolomite’

item.title: India's Industrial production growth likely to grow 4.3% in March: Union Bank Report
item.description: The report said that the increase in IIP was mainly driven by a pickup in manufacturing and mining activity

item.title: India’s biofuel future: Sugar-based ethanol’s diminishing role and rise of multi-feedstock adaptation
item.description: It will remain a contributor to India’s biofuel mix, but its dominance is over

item.title: Worst of US tariff impact, uncertainty behind us: Prashant Khemka, founder & MD of WhiteOak Capital
item.description: US tariffs can be a boon to Indian manufacturing beyond the initial transient period of pain, Prashant Khemka, founder & MD of WhiteOak Capital, says

item.title: Palm oil farming, a transformative economic force for India
item.description: The country’s palm oil mission is more than agricultural reform—it’s a blueprint for inclusive growth, economic resilience, and sustainability

item.title: DGCA issues advisory to airlines on passenger handling due to longer flights
item.description: Pakistan has closed its airspace for Indian airlines, resulting in longer flying hours for their international flights, especially those flying out from north Indian cities, including Delhi

item.title: India Inc cautious amid global headwinds: BL Survey
item.description: Most businesses see moderate impact from US tariff measures

item.title: Indian commercial vehicle industry gears up for green, tech-driven future
item.description: Despite past challenges like economic downturns and regulatory shifts, strong recovery factors such as urbanisation, infrastructure growth, and favourable policies have set the stage for rapid advancements

item.title: Direct Tax collection missed revised estimates
item.description: Meanwhile, corporate tax collection exceeded RE

item.title: US-India trade talks advance with focus on e-commerce, farm goods, data storage
item.description: The terms of reference for the deal—finalised this week—span trade in both goods and services, signalling a broad effort to strengthen bilateral economic ties

item.title: Wheat procurement nears 20 million tonnes, up 46%
item.description: The daily procurement on April 24 was 1.47 mt, as against 1.67 mt year-ago

item.title: Tech adoption can raise cotton productivity, says SABC study
item.description: Drip irrigation, fertigation and IPM can boost yields significantly

item.title: India orders Pakistani nationals without long-term visas to leave by April 27
item.description: The directive affects journalists, artists, students, and businessmen, while those on medical visas must leave by April 29

item.title: Indian goods worth $10 billion reach Pakistan annually via transshipment, GTRI report finds
item.description: These goods are exported from India to third countries like Dubai, Singapore, and Colombo, where they are stored in bonded warehouses and re-labeled to mask their Indian origin

item.title: India’s onshore wind power market may hit record capacity in 2025: GWEC
item.description: India’s onshore wind market is projected to maintain strong growth in the coming years

item.title: SEA urges Food Ministry to hike import duty on RBD palmolein, refined palm oil to 40%
item.description: SEA president Asthana says lower duty differential between RBD palmolein and CPO hurting refining sector

item.title: Pakistan airspace closure: Airlines’ deploy strategies to cut losses
item.description: Airlines focus on technical stops, alternative routes and facilities

item.title: ‘Collaboration essential to overcome poultry sector challenges’
item.description: The Indian poultry industry is at a critical crossroads, where timely interventions and strategic collaborations are essential to overcome the challenges posed by rising feed costs, supply chain disruptions, and market volatility, said Divya Kumar Gulati, Chairman, CLFMA of India

item.title: Orthodox tea prices gain at Kochi auctions on export demand
item.description: Overseas buyers showed interest on surge in buying after Easter holidays

item.title: IMF says Asian central banks can cut rates to tackle trade war impact
item.description: Krishna Srinivasan, Director of the IMF’s Asia and Pacific Department, noted inflation is within or below target ranges, allowing for monetary easing despite the risk of currency depreciation

item.title: India considers 49% FDI in nuclear power; legal reforms likely in July
item.description: The move aims to accelerate the country’s clean energy transition and reach a target of 100 GW nuclear capacity by 2047.

item.title: India cuts post-harvest losses, eyes global leadership in food processing
item.description: Speaking at the SUFALAM event hosted by NIFTEM-Kundli, Paswan underlined the sector’s role in achieving India’s vision of becoming a developed nation by 2047

item.title: Sri Lanka to begin talks with India, UAE for new energy hub
item.description: The hub in the harbour city of Trincomalee will involve the construction of a multi-product pipeline as well as bunkering facilities and potentially a refinery.

item.title: APSEZ’s acquisition of Australian terminal credit neutral: Fitch Ratings
item.description: Acquisition to increase share of APSEZ’s EBITDA from global operations to 10% from 4% currently, Fitch Ratings says

item.title: Finimin notifies rules for filing, management, adjudication of appeals before GSTAT
item.description: These are structured across 11 chapters, 70 rules, and 4 prescribed forms; The framework ensures uniformity, judicial discipline, and digital readiness across the Tribunal’s functioning

item.title: Air India in talks with Boeing to take 10 planes snubbed by Chinese airlines, sources say
item.description: Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg said on Wednesday that "due to the tariffs, many of our customers in China have indicated that they will not take delivery", and the company could redirect jets to other customers in need

item.title: As India halts trade, Pakistan may seek Indian goods via third countries at higher prices: GTRI
item.description: The border closure is expected to stop only formal trade, not demand

item.title: Govt to work on facilitating FPOs to get the benefit of margins charged by intermediaries
item.description: Issue comes into focus at Agriculture Ministry’s webinar to discuss sector’s issues

item.title: Pahalgam fallout: Pakistan airspace closure to hurt India-based airlines
item.description: ‘Air India and IndiGo to be most affected by the closure of Pakistan’s airspace’

item.title: India lifts 17 crore out of poverty, employment growth outpaces population: World Bank
item.description: In its report titled India Poverty and Equity Brief, the multilateral agency said that extreme poverty—defined as living on less than $2.15 per day—fell to 2.3 per cent of the population in 2022–23, down from 16.2 per cent in 2011–12.

item.title: Upgrade and expand with a future-ready mindset, Modi tells steel industry
item.description: Urging the steel companies to focus on manufacturing high-grade steel, Modi stressed on the need for a goal of “zero imports” and a focus on net exports

item.title: India open to changing its electric vehicle policy to attract global companies
item.description: May work on tariff rates keeping in mind bilateral trade agreement with the US: Govt sources

item.title: India could be the first country to sign trade deal: US Treasury Secretary
item.description: ‘India does not have so many high tariffs,’ says US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent

item.title: Stainless steel cos seek anti-dumping duty amid global trade turbulence
item.description: Express concern that China may flood the Indian market with excess production

item.title: India’s fertiliser stocks at 3-year low, may pose problems to meet kharif demand
item.description: Estimate for the monsoon sowing season demand has been pegged 3% higher at 330.3 lakh tonnes

item.title: Storage in three-fourths of key Indian reservoir below 50% of capacity
item.description: Overall storage down to 35.5 per cent of capacity; Tamil Nadu boasts of best storage

item.title: ISRI, Zuari Industries sign MoU to implement ‘Cane Excellence Programme’
item.description: Project will enhance sugarcane productivity, sustainability, farmer engagement and R&D

item.title: Akums Drugs signs MoA with TNAU to boost R&D in agri-based pharma formulations
item.description: The collaboration will also offer industrial training and internships, fostering hands-on learning for students in formulation and processing.

item.title: India International Coffee Festival in Bengaluru from Friday
item.description: India International Coffee Festival 2025 in Bengaluru with over 100 stalls, championships and 10-year permission from Coffee Board

item.title: Avaana Capital leads seed round of ₹32 cr in insect protein firm GreenGrahi
item.description: The ₹32 crore raise will be used to build India’s largest BSF bio-factory, capable of processing 150 tonnes of feedstock daily

item.title: Bought leaf tea manufacturers mull nation-wide union to highlight their cause
item.description: Body to be known as Federation of Indian Tea Manufacturers Association

item.title: Extremely heavy rain lashes East India as more wet weather forecast for East and South
item.description: Prevailing wester disturbance drives pre-monsoon as fresh disturbance is expected soon

item.title: Safety assessment rating for trucks, heavy vehicles on lines of Bharat NCAP soon: Gadkari
item.description: The minister said the government is also working on reducing logistics cost to 9% in the next couple of years from the present 14-16%

item.title: India Inc revenue likely up 5-6% in Q4, says Crisil
item.description: According to Crisil , in consumer staples, the FMCG segment is expected to see 4-6% revenue growth led by price hikes amid subdued volume growth

item.title: Udupi CSL deliver first cargo ship to Norwegian company
item.description: Udupi-CSL delivers emission-free 3800 DWT vessel to Wilson Ship Management, part of Norway’s green funding program

item.title: PM Modi flags off four trains, launches projects worth ₹13,500 crore for Bihar
item.description: At an event to mark National Panchayati Raj Day, Modi also inaugurated the Supaul Pipra rail line, Hasanpur Bithan Rail line and two-lane rail over bridges at Chapra and Bagaha.

item.title: Telangana farmers to have access to crop insurance after a gap of 5 years
item.description: The scheme would provide farmers relief for crop loss caused by weather changes, particularly unseasonal rains, excessive rainfall, hailstorms, and drought conditions

item.title: Government waives upfront payment, offers rebates to expedite underground coal mining
item.description: In a decisive step towards revitalising India's coal sector, the Ministry of Coal has introduced a series of transformative policy measures aimed at promoting underground coal mining

item.title: India firms up gas projects with expected capacity of 3.5 bcm/year in 2024
item.description: Final investments in 21 projects reflect upstream confidence and supply-side recalibration

item.title: India’s decision to suspend Indus Water Treaty will impact Pakistan in four major ways
item.description: Its production of rice and cotton could be affected badly, says trade analyst

item.title: Tariffs could shave half percentage point off growth: Finance Secretary Seth
item.description: However, a more significant problem would be the global economy slumping, Ajay Seth said

item.title: Boeing ready to resell China-bound jets if trade war worsens
item.description: ‘China has stopped taking deliveries from Boeing, after the company reported better-than-expected results’

item.title: Integration of mandis with eNAM helping farmers get better prices
item.description: It is a huge advantage for growers as they can see prices on eNAM scroll, says BITS Goa assistant professor

item.title: Rallis India Q4FY25 loss widens to ₹32 crore on marginal dip in revenues and higher expense
item.description: FY25 profits lower at ₹125 crore on revenue of ₹2663 cr; Company declares dividend ₹2.5 per share

item.title: India becomes third largest contributor to consumer product growth among emerging markets: Report
item.description: Indian affiliates of top MNCs generate 2-6 times higher shareholder returns than their global parents, finds Bain and Company report

item.title: US’ nod to ToRs for trade pact with India sets the stage for trade talks in Washington
item.description: USTR’s fresh emphasis on ‘prohibitive tariffs’ on farm products may make the going tough for Indian team visiting US

item.title: Empower farmers holistically through innovative use of biologicals: Syngenta India
item.description: Syngenta Biologicals is an emerging vertical under the larger umbrella of Syngenta Global, says MD Susheel Kumar

item.title: RSS affiliate BKS wants a full stop on cultivation of GM crops
item.description: Calls for a permanent halt to their promotion in India, seeks assured procurement

item.title: Govt to supply 65 million tonnes of coal via rail-sea-rail route in FY26
item.description: The move comes amid govt push to promote multimodal transportation for a more efficient, resilient and sustainable coal supply chain
